description: city and county seat of Cook County, and largest city in State of Illinois, United States of America
description_wiki: Chicago is the most populous city in the U.S. state of Illinois and in the Midwestern United States.

description: company
description_wiki: Crane Co.

description: U.S. Army facility at Rockford, Illinois
description_wiki: Camp Grant was a U.S. Army facility located in the southern outskirts of Rockford, Illinois, named in honor of American Civil War general Ulysses S. Grant.

description: city in and county seat of Solano County, California, United States
description_wiki: Fairfield is a city in and the county seat of Solano County, California, United States, in the North Bay sub-region of the San Francisco Bay Area.

description: ocean between Asia, Australia and the Americas
description_wiki: The Pacific Ocean is the largest and deepest of Earth's five oceanic divisions.

aliases: Territory of New Caledonia and Dependencies
description: French special collectivity and archipelago in Oceania
description_wiki: New Caledonia is a French territory comprising a group of islands in the southwest Pacific Ocean, 220 km (140 mi) southwest of Vanuatu and 1,210 km (750 mi) east of Australia.

description: island in the Pacific Ocean
description_wiki: New Guinea (Tok Pisin: Niugini; Hiri Motu: Niu Gini; Indonesian: Papua, fossilized Nugini, also known as Papua or historically Irian) is the world's second-largest island, with an area of 785,753 km2 (303,381 sq mi).

description: place in Oro, Papua New Guinea
description_wiki: Buna is a village in Oro Province, Papua New Guinea.

description: 1917-1967 United States Army formation
description_wiki: The United States 32nd Infantry Division was formed from Army National Guard units from Wisconsin and Michigan and fought primarily during World War I and World War II. With roots as the Iron Brigade in the American Civil War, the division's ancestral units came to be referred to as the Iron Jaw Division.

description: 1942 World War II battle in the Pacific Theatre
description_wiki: The battle of Buna–Gona was part of the New Guinea campaign in the Pacific theatre during World War II. It followed the conclusion of the Kokoda Track campaign and lasted from 16 November 1942 until 22 January 1943.

description: 1917-1968 United States Army formation
description_wiki: The 33rd Infantry Division was a formation of the U.S. Army National Guard between 1917 and 1968.

description: town in Papua New Guinea
description_wiki: Aitape is a small town of about 18,000 people on the north coast of Papua New Guinea in the Sandaun Province.

description: village on the north coast of New Guinea
description_wiki: Saidor is a village located in Saidor ward of Rai Coast Rural LLG, Madang Province, on the north coast of Papua New Guinea.

description: Wikimedia disambiguation page
description_wiki: Hollandia may refer to:

description: island in Morotai Island Regency, North Maluku Province, Indonesia
United States Army general in WWI, WWII and Korea (1880–1964)
description_wiki: Douglas MacArthur (26 January 1880 – 5 April 1964) was an American general who served as a top commander during World War II and the Korean War, achieving the rank of General of the Army.

description: Dutch colony in Southeast Asia and Oceania (1800–1949)
description_wiki: The Dutch East Indies, also known as the Netherlands East Indies, was a Dutch colony with territory mostly comprising the modern state of Indonesia, which declared independence on 17 August 1945.

description: island in the Philippines
description_wiki: Leyte ( LAY-tee, LAY-tay, Tagalog: [ˈleite]) is an island in the Visayas group of islands in the Philippines.

description: landing craft
military unit
description_wiki: The 126th Infantry Regiment ("Second Michigan") is a United States military unit of the Michigan Army National Guard.

description: global war originating in Europe, 1914–1918
description_wiki: World War I, or the First World War (28 July 1914 – 11 November 1918), also known as The Great War, was a global conflict between two coalitions: the Allies (or Entente) and the Central Powers.

description: semi-automatic rifle
description_wiki: The M1 Carbine (formally the United States Carbine, Caliber .30, M1) is a lightweight semi-automatic carbine chambered in the .30 carbine (7.62×33mm) cartridge that was issued to the U.S. military during World War II, the Korean War, and the Vietnam War.
